zh.Lewes Priory, Lewes, Sussex, England: 英國素可列維斯列維斯 聖 多馬 亞特 克利夫
zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont於1317年12月25日出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris47歲，母親Alice Comyn28歲。他於1330年11月6日與Eleanor of Lancaster結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1338年被列為knight 。他去世於1342年4月14日在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年24歲，埋葬在英國素可列維斯列維斯 聖 多馬 亞特 克利夫。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont was born on 25 December 1317, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, was 47 and his mother, Alice Comyn, was 28. He married Eleanor of Lancaster on 6 November 1330. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1338, at the age of 20, his occupation is listed as knight . He died on 14 April 1342, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 24, and was buried in Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, England.

fr.Lewes Priory, Lewes, Sussex, England: Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont est né le 25 décembre 1317, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Alice Comyn, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor of Lancaster le 6 novembre 1330. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1338, à l’âge de 20 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est knight . Il est décédé le 14 avril 1342, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 24 ans, et a été enterré à Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.Lewes Priory, Lewes, Sussex, England: Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, England
de.lifeSummary: Als John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont am 25. Dezember 1317 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris 47 und seine Mutter Alice Comyn 28. Er hat am 6. November 1330 Eleanor of Lancaster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1338, als er 20 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als knight  aufgeführt. Er starb am 14. April 1342 im Alter von 24 Jahren in Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont è nato il il 25 dicembre 1317 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Alice Comyn, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor of Lancaster il 6 novembre 1330. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1338, all'età di 20 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come knight . Morì il 14 aprile 1342, nel Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 24 anni, e fu sepolto a Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumontが1317年12月25日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxorisは47歳，母Alice Comynは28歳であった。1330年11月6日にEleanor of Lancasterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1338年，20歳の時，職業はknight と記録されている。彼は1342年4月14日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで24歳で死去し，イギリススセックスルスルス スト ソマス アト クリフにて埋葬された。

ko.Lewes Priory, Lewes, Sussex, England: 영국 수세스 루스 루스 스트 톰아스 앳 클리프
ko.lifeSummary: John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont(이)가 1317년 12월 25일 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Comyn(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1330년 11월 6일 Eleanor of Lancaster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1338년, 20세의 나이로 그의 직업은 knight 이었다. 그는 1342년 4월 14일 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 24세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 루스 루스 스트 톰아스 앳 클리프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont nasceu em 25 de dezembro de 1317, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Alice Comyn, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor of Lancaster em 6 de novembro de 1330. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1338, com 20 anos, sua profissão aparece como knight . Ele faleceu em 14 de abril de 1342, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 24 anos, e foi sepultado em Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.Lewes Priory, Lewes, Sussex, England: львесь ст томас ат клифф, львесь, суссекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont родился 25 декабря 1317 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Comyn, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor of Lancaster 6 ноября 1330 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1338 году, в возрасте 20 лет,  он работал по профессии knight . Он умер 14 апреля 1342 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 24 лет и был похоронен в местности львесь ст томас ат клифф, львесь, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Beaumont 2nd Lord Beaumont nació el 25 de diciembre de 1317, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Alice Comyn, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Eleanor of Lancaster el 6 de noviembre de 1330. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1338, a la edad de 20 años, su ocupación registrada era knight . Murió el 14 de abril de 1342, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 24 años, y fue sepultado en Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.
